forensic_flags.narrative: Free cash flow was -$680M in FY2025 (OCF minus capex). Sustained negative FCF is the clock on the wall — it sets how fast liquidity is consumed and how soon a refinancing or restructuring is forced. At the FY2025 burn rate, reported cash alone covers ~19 months.
forensic_flags.pointer: Read the liquidity & capital-resources MD&A, revolver availability/borrowing base, and the maturity schedule.

amount.note: This is a related-party (20.8% equity interest) recurring capacity purchase commitment. The $658M figure is an annual income-statement expense, not a balance-sheet obligation or total future commitment, so it is not an incremental debt amount to add to the bridge. Recorded for informational completeness; no total future minimum commitment is quantified in the excerpt.

covenants.leverage_covenant_type: None (no leverage-ratio maintenance covenant). This secured facility instead relies on a Collateral Coverage Ratio maintenance test and a Liquidity covenant (Sections 6.08 and 6.09).
covenants.leverage_ratio_threshold: No leverage ratio. Collateral Coverage Ratio must be at least 1.6 to 1.0: a "Collateral Coverage Ratio Failure" is defined as failure of the ratio "to be at least equal to 1.6 to 1.0." (Collateral Coverage Ratio = Appraised Value of Collateral / Total Obligations.)
ebitda_addback_categories: Extraordinary losses and net losses on Dispositions of assets
Provision for income/profit taxes
Fixed Charges (interest expense)
Foreign currency translation losses (including on Indebtedness remeasurement)
Depreciation, amortization of intangibles and other non-cash charges
Amortization of debt discount
Equity-based compensation / grants of Equity Interests to employees
Net loss on sale/disposition of capital assets (including inventory and securities sold with fixed assets)
Fuel hedging losses (pre-Closing arrangements and realized post-Closing losses)
Business interruption insurance proceeds
Expenses/charges covered by indemnification or reimbursement in connection with permitted acquisitions, mergers, dispositions, debt incurrence, equity issuance or investments
Other non-cash items increasing Consolidated Net Income (excluding ordinary-course revenue accruals)
covenants.restricted_payments_basket_size: Section 6.01 (Restricted Payments) governs; the specific basket dollar amounts/builder amounts are not set out in the excerpted text provided, so the RP basket size cannot be quoted verbatim from the pages furnished.
covenants.mfn_sunset_period: Not addressed in the excerpted text. The MFN concept appears only via the "All-In Initial Yield" definition used for incremental facility yield calculation; no MFN protection level or sunset period is stated in the provided pages.
covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: The agreement expressly contemplates Restricted/Unrestricted Subsidiary designation via Section 5.05 ("Designation of Restricted and Unrestricted Subsidiaries"). Consolidated Net Income excludes "the net income (loss) of any Unrestricted Subsidiary," so earnings and assets can be moved outside the credit group by designating Unrestricted Subsidiaries. The detailed conditions/caps on designation are in Section 5.05, which is not reproduced in the excerpt.
covenants.lme_risk_notes: This is the Eleventh Amendment to American Airlines' A&R Credit and Guaranty Agreement (SGR/route-authority-collateralized facility), establishing $362,833,333.30 of 2026 Incremental Revolving Commitments and $155,000,000 of new LC Commitments, with Barclays as Administrative Agent and Citi added as arranger. Key LME-relevant features: (1) The amendment contains a pre-baked serial-amendment/"Specified Terms" mechanism — the 2026 Incremental Revolving Lenders irrevocably consent in advance to a future "Specified Amendment" and agree no further consent or signature is required, with effectiveness on Required Lenders' consent (taking their pre-given consent into account) or upon repayment/repricing/replacement of the 2024 Replacement Term Loans. This is an uptier-style consent-harvesting structure that reduces minority-lender protection. (2) Protection here is collateral-based rather than leverage-based: value is governed by the 1.6x Collateral Coverage Ratio (Appraised Value of Collateral / Total Obligations, where Total Obligations includes revolver exposure, all Term Loans, Pari Passu Senior Secured Debt, and designated hedging/banking product obligations) plus a Liquidity covenant, with an Officer's Certificate condition certifying compliance. (3) Unrestricted Subsidiary designation (Section 5.05) plus CNI exclusion of Unrestricted Subsidiary income creates drop-down/asset-transfer capacity, but the excerpt does not reproduce the Section 5.05/6.04 (Disposition of Collateral) conditions needed to assess a J.Crew-style IP/asset drop-down blocker. (4) No maintenance leverage covenant, no stated MFN sunset in the excerpt.

citation.quote: "'Collateral Coverage Ratio Failure' shall mean, as of any date of determination, the failure of the Collateral Coverage Ratio as of such date to be at least equal to 1.6 to 1.0."

covenants.leverage_covenant_type: None (no total-leverage maintenance covenant); the deal instead uses a Collateral Coverage Ratio and a minimum Liquidity covenant as its maintenance financial covenants
covenants.leverage_ratio_threshold: No leverage-based threshold. Maintenance tests are: (i) Collateral Coverage Ratio must be at least 1.6 to 1.0 (a "Collateral Coverage Ratio Failure" is failure to be at least 1.6:1.0), tested per Section 6.09; and (ii) minimum Liquidity per Section 6.08.
ebitda_addback_categories: Extraordinary losses and net losses on dispositions of assets
Provision for income/profit taxes
Fixed Charges
Foreign currency translation losses (including on Indebtedness remeasurement)
Depreciation, amortization (including of intangibles) and other non-cash charges/expenses
Amortization of debt discount
Non-cash equity/stock grant compensation to employees
Net losses on sale/exchange/disposition of capital assets
Losses under fuel hedging arrangements
Business interruption insurance proceeds
Expenses/charges covered by indemnification or reimbursement in connection with permitted acquisitions, mergers, dispositions, debt incurrence, equity issuance or investments
covenants.restricted_payments_basket_size: Section 6.01 ("Restricted Payments") governs RPs, but the specific builder/general basket dollar amounts are not set out in the excerpted text and cannot be stated verbatim.
covenants.mfn_sunset_period: Not present in the provided text. The definition of "All-In/Initial Yield" references OID and Adjusted Term SOFR floor mechanics for Incremental Term Loans, but no MFN margin-protection provision or its sunset is set out in the excerpted pages.
covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Section 5.05 governs "Designation of Restricted and Unrestricted Subsidiaries"; Consolidated Net Income excludes net income (loss) of any Unrestricted Subsidiary, confirming a restricted/unrestricted subsidiary construct exists. The full designation mechanics and any investment/value caps are not contained in the excerpted text and cannot be quantified from the provided pages.
covenants.lme_risk_notes: This is a Fourth Amendment establishing $1,451,333,333.35 of 2026 Incremental Revolving Commitments (replacing terminated existing revolver/LC commitments) for American Airlines, Inc. / American Airlines Group Inc.; it is a secured facility (guaranties plus Collateral, Collateral Trustee, SGR Security Agreement covering Route Authorities, Slots, Foreign Gate Leaseholds). Key structural feature: obligations are secured on a defined Collateral pool with a hard 1.6:1.0 Collateral Coverage Ratio maintenance test plus a minimum Liquidity covenant (Sections 6.08/6.09), so priming/drop-down risk turns primarily on collateral leakage rather than a leverage covenant. LME watch-items: (1) Section 5.05 unrestricted-subsidiary designation combined with CNI excluding Unrestricted Subsidiary income creates a J.Crew-style drop-down channel — the presence/absence of an IP/asset-transfer blocker is NOT determinable from the excerpted pages (set to null). (2) Incremental facility capacity is expressly contemplated (Section 2.27 incremental mechanics; Pari Passu Senior Secured Debt is included in the Collateral Coverage Ratio denominator), indicating the structure permits additional pari passu secured debt against the same collateral — a potential dilution/priming vector. (3) No leverage-based maintenance covenant and no observable MFN protection in the provided text; verification of RP baskets, uptier/sacred-rights amendment provisions and J.Crew blocker requires the full Credit Agreement (Sections 6.01, 6.04, 9.x) not included here.

citation.quote: "Collateral Coverage Ratio Failure" shall mean, as of any date of determination, the failure of the Collateral Coverage Ratio as of such date to be at least equal to 1.6 to 1.0.
